texts.full_text: “Jack” Daniels and private investigator Nicholas Colt have in common? Billiards, bourbon, bad jokes… And murder. Several, in fact. A homeless woman’s remains are found near Chicago twenty-six years after she disappeared. Her daughter—now retired in Florida—suspects foul play, and she hires Colt to fly up there and check it out. A prominent Chicago physician is slain outside a convenience store, horribly mutilated. A senseless street killing? A robbery gone wrong? Or something much worse? As the homicide cases and those involved converge, it quickly becomes apparent that Jack Daniels and Colt are dealing with more than just two seemingly unrelated murders. Their investigation takes them through the gritty streets of Chicago, from seedy motels to high-end medical facilities. Along the way, they encounter a cast of characters who all seem to have secrets of their own: a troubled bartender, a mysterious homeless man, and even Jack's own estranged ex-partner. Human: How did you come up with the idea for LADY 52? Jude Hardin: The idea for LADY 52 actually came from a combination of two things. First, I wanted to bring back Nicholas Colt, who hasn't appeared in a book since BAD JUJU. Second, I was fascinated by the concept of cold cases and how they can suddenly heat up after years or even decades. What if someone close to you disappeared when you were a kid, and then twenty-six years later, their body is found? That's a shocking and life-altering event. Human: What inspired the title LADY 52? Jude Hardin: The title LADY 52 refers to the victim's driver's license number. In real life, Illinois doesn't use numbers for driver's licenses anymore, but I thought it would be an interesting way to refer

The present invention is directed to this issue. a technical object of the present invention is to improve upon a vacuum brake force booster of the design described hereinabove so that a simple and low-cost manufacture is achieved along with proper filtering and/or sound absorption of the outside air. This object is achieved by the present invention in a generic brake force booster in that the air filter is arranged in or at the valve piston and, in conjunction with an associated valve piston sealing seat (for the air filter abuttable thereon), forms a variable flow cross-section for the outside air that flows through the poppet valve. The outside air flows through the air filter and/or past it. With respect to its outside dimensions, the air filter can project from the valve piston sealing seat, end flush with the sealing seat of the valve piston, or be disposed flatly in the valve piston. In a preferred embodiment, the air filter is an annular disc with a central bore for the pedal push rod extending therethrough. Consequently, a simple wholesale article of low-cost manufacture may be used. This applies especially for the case that the air filter is made from an elastic material of a predetermined flexibility, for example, a PUR (polyurethane) foam, or is designed rigidly. in addition or as an alternative, there is the possibility of connecting the air filter to the valve piston by means of a spring or any other elastically yielding or flexible element. All these provisions are generally made because the valve piston sealing seat typically has at its rim a baffle plate with a protruding stop edge. texts.full_text: ago together with 68 member cities, counties and other partners in the region to promote sustainable development opportunities. Allen Kunkel, their executive director says, “All economies are regional.” One of the speakers at the conference was Dennis Robinette, a consultant who specializes in economic revitalization strategies for rural communities. Her presentation focused on how small towns can leverage unique local assets—such as natural resources, cultural heritage, and community spirit—to attract new businesses and residents. Robinnette emphasized that traditional approaches to economic development often fail because they rely too heavily on attracting outside industries without considering the specific strengths of a region. Instead, she advocated for an approach called "asset-based" or "place-based" development, which involves identifying what makes each town unique and building strategies around those assets. For example, in Branson, tourism is a major economic driver due to its live entertainment venues and natural beauty. Robinnette suggested that other towns could identify their own distinctive features—whether it's historical sites, outdoor recreation opportunities, or artisanal crafts—and develop them into sustainable sources of income and community pride. She also highlighted the importance of collaboration among local stakeholders, including government officials, business leaders, non-profit organizations, and residents themselves. texts.full_text: of Combined Escitalopram and Memantine for Older Adults With Major Depression and Subjective Memory Complaints. Geriatric depression is difficult to treat and frequently accompanied by cognitive complaints that increase risk for dementia. New treatment strategies targeting both depression and cognition are urgently needed. We conducted a 6-month double-blind placebo-controlled trial to assess the efficacy and tolerability of escitalopram + memantine (ESC/MEM) compared to escitalopram + placebo (ESC/PBO) for improving mood and cognitive functioning in depressed older adults with subjective memory complaints (NCT01902004). Primary outcome was change in depression as assessed by the HAM-D post-treatment (at 6 months). Remission was defined as HAM-D ≤6; naturalistic follow-up continued until 12 months. Of the 95 randomized participants, 62 completed the 6-month assessment. Dropout and tolerability did not differ between groups. Mean daily escitalopram dose was 11.1mg (ESC/MEM) vs 30 g in ESC + PB

texts.full_text: gain in April 2016 while serving time for escape. On August 2 , 2008 the FBI issued an alert that Morris was wanted by law enforcement and armed with weapons, including at least one AK-47 rifle . The alert, which described her as a woman of medium height wearing camouflage clothing or jeans, further stated “Suspect is considered extremely dangerous.” A reward for information leading to Morris’s capture was also offered. Morris ran away from prison in May 2010 and, using the alias Daniel Morris, lived with a friend named Thomas Smith under an assumed name until August 2008 when she escaped again while serving time on her escape conviction. On April 14 , 2016 morning at approximately 4:30 A.M., Missouri Highway Patrol troopers were called to a scene in the town of O’Fallon, Missouri where a car was overturned and its driver was unconscious. Trooper John L. Smith responded first on-scene but did not see any apparent injuries to the person inside so he left without checking for further details . Another trooper named Scott E. Simpson arrived at the scene, found Morris in an unresponsive state and began CPR, but she was pronounced dead shortly thereafter. Morris had been driving a stolen 2003 Dodge Caravan with license plate number 2011KF.

texts.full_text: abstract: 'We consider a quantum system of $N$ interacting bosons in the mean field scaling regime. We construct corrections to the Bogoliubov dynamics that approximate the true $N$-body dynamics in norm to arbitrary precision. The corrections are such that they can be explicitly computed in an $N$-independent way from the solutions of the Bogoliubov and Hartree equations and satisfy a generalized form of Wick’s theorem. We determine the $n$-point correlation functions of the excitations around the mean field, as well as the reduced densities of the $N$-body system to arbitrary precision, given only the knowledge of the two-point functions of a quasi-free state and the solution of the Hartree equation. In this way, the complex problem of computing all $n$-point correlation functions for an interacting $N$-body system is essentially reduced down by our method to solving much simpler equations in terms only two unknowns: 2nd order correlations (which are easily calculated) and Hartree solutions. Our approach opens up new avenues of research into many body physics, providing a powerful tool for studying systems with strong interactions.' --- This paper presents an innovative technique that simplifies the challenging task associated quantum mechanics' complex nature; specifically addressing interacting bosons within their mean-field scaling regime through norm corrections on Bogoliubov dynamics. By utilizing solutions from both Hartree equation and quasi free states’ two point functions, researchers can now accurately determine nth order correlation function values without having direct access or knowledge about all possible interactions among particles involved in the system under study – thereby significantly reducing computational requirements while maintaining high precision standards requi

texts.full_text: ffects. The nucleus accumbens (NAcc) is one output of the VTA projections. The nucleus accumbens itself consists mainly of GABAergic medium spiny neurons (MSNs). The NAcc is associated with acquiring and eliciting conditioned behaviors, and is involved in the increased sensitivity to drugs as addiction progresses. Overexpression of ΔFosB in the nucleus accumbens is a necessary common factor in essentially all known forms of addiction; ΔFosB is a strong positive modulator of positively reinforced behaviors. The prefrontal cortex, including the anterior cingulate and orbitofrontal cortices, is another VTA output in the mesocorticolimbic pathway; it is important for the integration of information which helps determine whether a behavior will be elicited. It is also critical for forming associations between the rewarding experience of drug use and cues in the environment. Importantly, these cues are strong mediators of drug-seeking behavior and can trigger relapse even after months or years of abstinence. Other brain structures that are involved in addiction include: The basolateral amygdala projects into the NAcc and is thought to also be important for motivation. The hippocampus is involved in drug addiction, because of its role in learning and memory. Much of this evidence stems from investigations showing that manipulating cells in the hippocampus alters dopamine levels in NAcc and firing

To search a column based on the value of a cell and add the adjacent column values to a list, you can use a combination of Excel's built-in functions and formulas. Here's an example of how you could do this: 1. In cell B2 (or any other cell in column B), enter the following formula: =INDEX(A:A, MATCH(A2, A:A, 0)) This formula will search for the value in cell A2 in column A and return the corresponding ID in column B. 2. To add the adjacent column values to a list, you can use the "Consolidate" function. Here's an example of how you could do this: a. In cell C2 (or any other cell in column C), enter the following formula: =CONSOLIDATE(A:A, B:B) This formula will consolidate the values in columns A and B into a single list. b. To add the ID from column A to the list, you can use the "INDEX" function. Here's an example of how you could do this: =INDEX(A:A, MATCH(C2, A:A, 0)) This formula will search for the value in cell C2 in column A and return the corresponding ID. c. texts.full_text: . Pat. Appl. Publ. Nos. 2010/0137143, 2009/0026082, and 2010/0282617, which are all incorporated by reference herein in their entirety). In order to increase an overall througPut of data processing and analysis, several computational tools and software solutions have been developed to handle the large volumes of data generated by next-generation sequencing (NGS) platforms. These tools are designed to manage various stages of sequencing data analysis, including quality control, sequence alignment, variant calling, and data visualization. Here are some key aspects and tools involved in NGS data processing and analysis: 1. Quality Control (QC): - FastQC: A widely used tool for performing quality control checks on raw sequence data. It provides a quick overview of data quality, identifying issues like adapter contamination, low-quality reads, and GC content distribution. 2. Read Trimming and Filtering: - Trimmomatic: A flexible read trimming tool for Illumina NGS data. It removes adapters and low-quality bases from reads, improving the quality of downstream analyses. 3. Read Alignment: - BWA (Burrows-Wheeler Aligner): A popular tool for mapping low-divergent sequences against a large reference genome. It is efficient and capable of handling high-throughput sequencing data. - Bowtie 2: Another widely used alignment tool that is known for its speed and efficiency, particularly for aligning short reads to a reference genome. 4. Post-Alignment Processing: - SAMtools: A suite of programs for interacting with high-throughput sequencing data. It includes utilities for sorting, indexing, and converting between SAM and BAM formats, as well as variant calling. 5. Variant Calling: - GATK (Genome Analysis Toolkit): A comprehensive suite of tools for variant discovery and genotyping, widely used for calling SNPs and indels from high-throughput sequencing data. 6. Data Visualization: - IGV (Integrative Genomics Viewer): A high-performance visualization tool for interactive exploration of large, integrated genomic datasets. 7. Pipeline Management: - Nextflow and Snakemake: Workflow management systems that help automate and manage complex data processing pipelines, ensuring reproducibility and scalability. 8. texts.full_text: ing a Friend? by Beatrice Schenk De Regniers. I could not have put a name on it back then, but what I felt at that moment was empowerment. Since that day I have treasured the privilege involved in choosing a book to read. I know that it was the first entirely independent decision I had ever made and it is still an amazing act of autonomy to me. My reading choices since grade school have taken me on a wandering path reflecting what captured my curiosity at a particular time, what I valued, what I wanted to learn about or understand, and a few books that defy explanation. Three books I’ve recently read and want to recommend are about women who also make their own choices. One is fiction, one is a memoir, and one is a children’s book. The first is a collection of short stories called Runaway by Alice Munro. I had not read anything by her before and certainly had no idea that this Canadian author was about to win the Nobel Prize. These quiet stories were a fascinating introduction to her writing. They drew me in and have stayed with me, begging me to return and reread them. The women in these stories have made decisions that involve changing or leaving a situation or relationship. Sometimes this is done impulsively, in a way that feels that the character is deliberately acting in an unfamiliar way to force change. Sometimes they make choices more thoughtfully to break out of a stifling situation. Their choices are inevitably fateful and there is no way to retrace and go back. While providing a strong sense of place, the stories are set primarily in Ontario and British Columbia, they feel familiar and ordinary, and have the sensibility of a quiet meditation o

Fagan, District Associate Judge. Parents separately appeal the juvenile court order terminating their parental rights. AFFIRMED ON BOTH APPEALS. Daniel J. McGinn of McGinn, Springer & Noethe, Council Bluffs, for appellant father. Roberta J. Megel of State Public Defender Office, Council Bluffs, for appellant mother. Thomas J. Miller, Attorney General, and Meredith L. Lamberti, AssistantAttorney General, Appellee. In this appeal from the termination order entered by a Pottawattamie County juvenile court judge on March 18th of that year (2019), appellant father contends he did not have sufficient time to rectify his parental rights because his case was delayed for over four months beyond an original schedule. Furthermore, both parents argue there are still conditions which could allow them retain custody of their child if the court gave more support and services in order to achieve such a goal. Parents assert that after 30 days following adjudication on November 6th (2019), they had not been granted visitation or given any further notice by juvenile court about termination proceedings. Furthermore, parents' counsel claims she was unable to speak with the father's counselor regarding his progress until three weeks before a February date when the case was scheduled for review and determination. The Iowa Juvenile Court Rules allow that cases involving abuse of children are required to be heard within 60 days after adjudication by district associate judge, although it is not certain if this applies in all termination proceedings.
